title: Delicate Creamy Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A floral and comforting Moroccan dish featuring lamb, creamy sauce, and unique flavors, made flexitarian-friendly with the option to substitute lamb with portobello mushrooms, and incorporating Thai fish sauce for added depth, all cooked to perfection in a yakimono-grill and served in a traditional tagine form.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: or portobello mushrooms for flexitarian option

instructions: Preheat the yakimono-grill to medium-high heat, approximately 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, Thai fish sauce, cumin, and paprika to make the marinade.
Add the lamb shoulder or portobello mushrooms to the marinade, tossing to coat, and let it sit for at least 30 minutes.
Remove the lamb or mushrooms from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Grill the lamb or mushrooms for 5 minutes per side, or until they are browned and cooked to the desired level of doneness.
In a large tagine or Dutch oven, cook the chopped onion over medium heat until it is softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the tagine, cooking for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the heavy cream, dried apricots, and preserved lemon to the tagine, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and let cook for 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Add the grilled lamb or mushrooms to the tagine, stirring to coat with the creamy sauce.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs and crusty bread on the side.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
